items.title: UGREEN’s sleek new 2-in-1 Qi2.2 25W MagSafe charger is now just $40 with a launch deal
items.summary:
UGREEN’s official Amazon storefront is offering a nice launch deal on its all-new Qi2.2 25W 2-in-1 MagSafe charger. You can snag this latest model in both black and white finishes for $39.99 shipped. That works out to a 27% discount on its $55 list price it debuted with last month. This is essentially an upgraded Qi2.2 version of the 15W model we’ve featured previously, meaning you can now take advantage of faster 25W wireless charging speeds for quicker top-ups. It also sports a more distinctive design compared to many of the generic charging stands on the market, making it a nice option if you’re looking to add something a bit different to your desk or bedside setup.

items.title: AMD’s newest and fastest Ryzen 9850X3D gaming desktop processor just hit a new low at $459
items.summary:
We are now tracking a deal at Amazon that drops AMD’s latest Ryzen 7 9850X3D desktop processor down to $459 shipped. This is the newest chip in the brand’s X3D lineup, which hit the scene earlier this year carrying a $499 price tag. While you’re not looking at a massive discount on it right now, it’s still a noteworthy offer since it marks a new all-time low for the chip. It’s rare that we see AMD’s latest-generation processors receive meaningful discounts this soon after launch. The Ryzen 7 9800X3D, for instance, didn’t see its first major price cut until nearly a year after debuting. That makes today’s deal a notable one if you were already considering an upgrade to AMD’s newest gaming-focused CPU platform. Even saving a little here can free up extra room in your budget for smaller PC upgrades like additional case fans, storage, or peripherals such as a new keyboard or mouse. Head below for more details

items.summary:
Fanttik C8 Nano Electric Cardboard Scissors

Buy for $50 (Reg. $60)

Clip the on-page $10 off coupon.

What we love

In my city recycling is hauled away once a month, which can be a bit of a challenge unless we pack everything inside in an organized fashion. This rings especially true when we order something big off of Amazon and have a large box to fit in there. I usually use a utility knife to cut individual pieces that will fit inside nicely, but that’s a pain and why I’m strongly considering one of these for my own home.
